ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR BIFURCATION OF TRIAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE
ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF DEFENDANTS FIFTH, SIXTH, AND SEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS BARBARA PRICE, LARRY PRICE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF DEFENDANTS FIFTH, SIXTH, AND SEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. PLAINTIFFS HAVE MAINTAINED THEIR INITIAL SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BURDEN BY RELYING ON DEFENDANT'S FACTUALLY DEVOID RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1. PLAINTIFF ASKED FOR ALL FACTS THAT SUPPORT THE 5TH DEFENSE FOR INTERVENING/SUPERSEDING CAUSE, 6TH DEFENSE FOR FAILURE TO MITIGATE, AND 7TH DEFENSE FOR COMPARATIVE FAULT. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE WAS VAGUE AND EVASIVE. SEE UNION BANK V. SUP. CRT. (1995) 31 CAL.APP.4TH 573. IN OPPOSITION, DEFENDANT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING DEFENSES 5, 6, AND 7. DEFENDANT MAY BE ABLE TO ESTABLISH THE COMPARATIVE FAULT AND SUPERVENING CAUSE DEFENSES. AT 48:1-23; 51:4-12; 61:8-15 OF THE MCGINNES DEPO, HE STATES THAT PLAINTIFFS' WIRES CAUSED THE FIRE. AT 77:9-16, HE STATES THAT DEFENDANT'S MAIN PANEL WAS INTACT AND DID NOT EXPERIENCE DAMAGE. DEFENDANT MAY ALSO BE ABLE TO ESTABLISH THE FAILURE TO MITIGATE DEFENSE. AT 10:4-18 AND 102:4-15, BARBARA PRICE ADMITS THAT NO CONSTRUCTION TO REBUILD HAS YET TO COMMENCE. MR. PRICE ADMITS THAT THE WEATHER RUINED CERTAIN FURNITURE (74:1-6) AND THAT HE QUIT HIS JOB BECAUSE OF THE FIRE (76:5-16; 72:21-73:22). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, PG&E CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR BIFURCATION OF TRIAL. MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE SOLE MODIFICATION OF SPECIFYING THAT THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT BIFURCATION WILL SUBSTANTIALLY BENEFIT THE COURT OR THE PARTIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

MINI MINUTES:

Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Defendants Fifth, Sixth, And Seventh Affirmative Defenses.
Off Calendar Dec-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SHAWN R. MILLER, ESQ. of Danko Meredith Trial Lawyers, Tel No. 650-453-3600 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. GAYLE L. GOUGH, ESQ. of Gough & Hancock LLP, Tel No. 415-848-8900 appearing for Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

RULING - Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFFS BARBARA PRICE, LARRY PRICE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Defendants Fifth, Sixth, And Seventh Affirmative Defenses, the court denies the motion for summary adjudication. Plaintiffs have maintained their initial summary adjudication burden by relying on defendant's factually devoid response to form interrogatory 15.1. Plaintiff asked for all facts that support the 5th defense for intervening/superseding cause, 6th defense for failure to mitigate, and 7th defense for comparative fault. Defendant's response was vague and evasive. See Union Bank v. Sup. Crt. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573. In opposition, defendant creates a triable issue of material fact regarding defenses 5, 6, and 7. Defendant may be able to establish the comparative fault and supervening cause defenses. At 48:1-23; 51:4-12; 61:8-15 of the McGinnes depo, he states that plaintiffs' wires caused the fire. At 77:9-16, he states that defendant's main panel was intact and did not experience damage. Defendant may also be able to establish the failure to mitigate defense. At 10:4-18 and 102:4-15, Barbara Price admits that no construction to rebuild has yet to commence. Mr. Price admits that the weather ruined certain furniture (74:1-6) and that he quit his job because of the fire (76:5-16; 72:21-73:22). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
